data.title: As a project maintainer, ADRs are always generated for architectural decisions without being asked
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Remove the C2 question about ADR preference and always generate ADRs for decisions made during the current session
**Why**: ADRs are an engineering best practice; asking whether to generate them invites users to opt out of a beneficial practice
**Scope**:
- IN: Remove Phase 1 Group C Question C2, always generate ADRs for session decisions, ADR scope is current session only (not historical)
- OUT: ADR idempotency on re-run (separate story), changing ADR format or content

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, architect-foundation generates ADRs for all architectural decisions made during the session without asking whether to generate them
  ← Satisfies: "ADRs are always generated for architectural decisions made during the current session"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Removing C2 changes ADR scope semantics — document that ADRs cover current session decisions only

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
